channel.title: Money Minute Daily
channel.description: Your free daily 5-minute finance podcast. Stock market, investing, personal finance, and economic news every morning. The smartest 5 minutes for your money.
channel.itunes:author: Money Minute Daily

item.title: Money News Jun 29 — Risks in Focus After US Stocks’ Wild Start to
item.description: The first half of 2026 has been nothing short of a wild ride for US stocks, and as we head into the second half of the year, investors are facing a daunting list of risks that could make or break their portfolios. From the rising tide of interest rates to the looming US elections and the unpredictable impact of artificial intelligence, it's a complex web of challenges that's keeping even the most seasoned investors on their toes. The fact that the Federal Reserve is likely to continue raising in...

item.title: Money News Jun 29 — PBOC’s New Overnight Rate Said Below Forecast
item.description: China's central bank just made a surprise move that could have major implications for the global economy, setting the interest rate on its new overnight liquidity tool lower than expected, effectively giving the market a de facto rate cut. This means that market borrowing costs could be pushed down, and some economists are interpreting this as a sign that China is looking to ease its monetary policy. Why does this matter? Well, a rate cut, or in this case, a de facto rate cut, can have a ripple ...

item.title: Money News Jun 30 — Quant funds just suffered their most cripplin
item.description: Quant funds are reeling after their worst trading rout of the year, with momentum stocks taking a beating in the last week, but before you hit the panic button, it's essential to take a step back and look at the bigger picture. Although the recent slump has been painful for quant funds, asset allocators are reminding investors that it's crucial to assess returns over a wider timeframe, and so far in 2026, the sector is still significantly ahead of the S&P 500 and U.S. Treasurys. This is why it m...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jun 30 — BlackRock Downgrades Emerging-Market Equities Over
item.description: Emerging markets, once the darling of investors, are taking a backseat as one of the world's largest asset managers sounds the alarm on AI risks. BlackRock Inc.'s research arm has become cautious on emerging-market stocks, and it's a significant shift in sentiment that matters to investors worldwide. The reason for this caution is the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence and its potential to disrupt traditional industries and economies. As AI continues to advance, the dynamics o...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jun 30 — Brazil Real Becomes ‘Collateral Damage’ as Dollar
item.description: The dollar is back and it's taking no prisoners, with the Brazilian real just becoming the latest casualty in its resurgence, recording its worst month this year as investors scramble to unwind their carry trades. This matters because it's a stark reminder of just how quickly sentiment can shift in the markets, and how even the most favored trades can become yesterday's news in the blink of an eye. The Brazilian real had been one of the darlings of emerging market currencies, but with the dollar...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jul 01 — Traders Boost Fed Funds Shorts in Risky July Rate-
item.description: Get ready for a potential market shake-up, as traders are placing a high-stakes wager that the Federal Reserve will start raising interest rates as soon as July. This move, once considered unthinkable, is now being bet on by traders in the fed funds market, who are ramping up their shorts in anticipation of a rate hike. But why does this matter? It's because this bold prediction is being made despite a series of economic data that could potentially derail it. The traders' reasoning is that the F...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jul 02 — Tech Stocks Retreat Deepens Ahead of US Payrolls:
item.description: Tech stocks are taking a beating, and it's not just a minor blip on the radar - the latest bout of volatility has entered its second day, with chipmakers in South Korea feeling the worst of it. This matters because the tech sector has been a major driver of market growth in recent years, so when it stumbles, investors take notice. The selling spree has also had a ripple effect on other assets, with the dollar falling and Treasuries wavering ahead of the highly anticipated June US payrolls report...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jul 03 — Trump’s billion-dollar crypto haul triggers some e
item.description: A billion-dollar crypto haul for one high-profile investor is putting the spotlight on the painful reality for millions of others who are still sitting on massive losses, and it's a stark reminder that the crypto market can be a wild ride. Former President Donald Trump's recent foray into cryptocurrency has reportedly yielded a whopping billion-dollar profit, but for many everyday investors, the story is very different. The ongoing bear market has erased trillions of dollars in paper profits, le...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jul 04 — Russia Said It Captured Key Ukraine Stronghold in
item.description: The world's financial landscape is shifting rapidly, and one of the most significant flashpoints is in Eastern Europe, where Russia has just announced the capture of a key Ukrainian stronghold in the Donetsk region. This strategic logistics hub is crucial for Ukraine's ability to respond to Russian aggression, and its fall could have far-reaching implications for the conflict. The fact that Russia is claiming full control of Ukraine's Luhansk region, just days after launching its deadliest attac...

item.title: Money Minute Daily | Jul 05 — BOK Warns of Risks From Single-Stock Leveraged ETF
item.description: Imagine waking up one morning to find your entire investment portfolio has been wiped out, thanks to a single bad bet on a trendy tech stock - that's the nightmare scenario the Bank of Korea is warning about when it comes to single-stock leveraged ETFs tied to giants like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. According to a recent report, these funds could amplify market volatility, exacerbate one-way trading flows, and deepen market concentration, making the entire system even more fragile....

item.title: Carry Traders Shift Away From Dollar in Emerging-Market Bets — Money Minute Daily | Jul 06
item.description: $1.4 trillion in emerging-market bets are being rewritten as traders ditch the US dollar in favor of the euro, Australian dollar, and other currencies to fund their investments in the developing world, a drastic shift that's sending shockwaves through the global currency market. This seismic change is largely driven by the US dollar's resurgence, which has made it more expensive for traders to use it as a funding currency, and as a result, they're looking for alternative currencies with lower bo...

item.title: Micron’s stock falls as investors wonder if the memory — Money Minute Daily | Jul 08
item.description: Micron's stock just plummeted 12% in a single day, leaving investors scrambling to figure out if the memory market has finally reached its peak. This sharp decline has many wondering if the party's over for one of the hottest tech sectors of the past year. The memory market, which has been fueled by the insatiable demand for AI-powered devices, has been on a tear lately, but it seems like investors are getting cold feet. According to one analyst, "Most investor feedback continues to point to a s...

item.title: Investors haven’t been this bullish on the dollar in a — Money Minute Daily | Jul 09
item.description: $114 billion, that's how much investors have pumped into bets on a stronger US dollar, making it the most bullish the market has been on the buck in a decade. What's behind this surge is a mix of factors, but mostly it's about the oil prices and the renewed Middle East tensions that are reviving inflation concerns. The jump in oil prices on Wednesday is a key factor here, as it's bolstering expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to keep policy tight. If oil prices keep climbing, it's lik...

item.title: Cencosud Shares Tumble as Strategic Plan Yet to Boost — Money Minute Daily | Jul 10
item.description: Cencosud's shares have plummeted 25% since the Chilean retailer unveiled its new strategic plan over a year ago, and investors are losing patience. The company's efforts to revamp its operations and boost earnings have yet to bear fruit, leaving many to wonder if the plan is working at all. This is a big deal because Cencosud is one of the largest retailers in South America, with a significant presence in Chile, Argentina, and Brazil, so its performance has a ripple effect on the entire region's...

item.title: SK Hynix Rises After Record US Offering — Money Minute Daily | Jul 11
item.description: SK Hynix just raised a whopping 2.5 billion dollars in its US debt offering, marking the largest deal of its kind for a South Korean company, and its stock is surging as a result. This massive influx of capital is a major coup for the memory chip giant, and it's likely to have significant implications for the global tech industry. The fact that SK Hynix was able to secure such a large offering is a testament to its strong financials and the continued demand for its products, despite the current ...

item.title: Big fast-food burger chain franchisee files Chapter 11 — Money Minute Daily | Jul 12
item.description: $13.2 billion in debt just evaporated from a big fast-food burger chain franchisee's balance sheet, as the company filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. This staggering number is more than just a headline, it's a wake-up call for the entire fast-food industry. The franchisee, which operates hundreds of locations, cited rising labor costs, increasing competition, and declining sales as the main reasons for its financial woes. This bankruptcy filing matters because it highlights the challenges facing t...

item.title: Gold and Silver Drop as Mideast Strikes Heighten Rate-Hike — Money Minute Daily | Jul 13
item.description: Gold prices plummeted by as much as 2.5% overnight, sparking a chain reaction that's now putting pressure on investors to reevaluate their portfolios amidst a fresh wave of geopolitical tensions. The US and Iran's exchange of strikes over the weekend has sent energy prices soaring, and that's got many experts predicting another round of interest-rate hikes to combat inflation. This is particularly significant because gold has long been seen as a safe-haven asset, a go-to for investors looking to...

item.title: US Stocks, Treasuries Steady Before Warsh and CPI: Markets — Money Minute Daily | Jul 14
item.description: $800 billion in market value has been shed by SpaceX in less than a month, sending shockwaves through the investment community and raising questions about the company's future prospects. This staggering loss is a stark reminder of the volatility of the stock market, where fortunes can be made and lost in a matter of weeks. As investors scramble to make sense of this dramatic downturn, many are left wondering if SpaceX's stock will fall below its IPO price, a milestone that could trigger a crisis...

item.title: Wall Street has set a sky-high bar for companies to clear — Money Minute Daily | Jul 15
item.description: $1.48 trillion is the staggering amount of earnings Wall Street expects companies to rake in this quarter, setting a sky-high bar that seems nearly impossible to clear. Yet, analysts at Piper Sandler think corporate America just might be able to pull it off. This is largely due to the impressive performance of the tech and finance sectors, which have been driving growth and innovation in recent months. If companies can indeed meet these lofty expectations, it would be a significant boost to the ...

item.title: ‘Arsenal of democracy’: Jamie Dimon announces $24 million — Money Minute Daily | Jul 16
item.description: Jamie Dimon just dropped $24 million to supercharge American shipbuilding with a brand-new submarine facility at the Philadelphia Navy Yard, sending a clear message that JPMorgan Chase is all in on boosting the country's naval capabilities. This massive investment is a strategic move to revitalize the US shipbuilding industry, which has been facing stiff competition from foreign players. By funding this state-of-the-art facility, Dimon is not only creating jobs but also positioning the US to reg...

item.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16 — Money Minute Daily | Jul 16
item.description: $930 million in commitments just poured into Balbec Capital's latest flagship fund, a staggering influx of cash for an asset-based credit fund that's now poised to make some big moves. This is a significant development because it shows that investors are still hungry for alternative assets, even in a market that's been shaken by rising interest rates and geopolitical tensions. The fact that Balbec was able to secure such a large amount of commitments suggests that the firm has a strong track rec...

item.title: Netflix is getting stingier about its viewing data, and — Money Minute Daily | Jul 17
item.description: Netflix's stock just plummeted 8% in a single day, and Wall Street is scrambling to understand the reasons behind this sudden drop. The main culprit seems to be the company's mixed earnings report, which failed to impress investors. But what's really got analysts worried is Netflix's new plan to cut back on the publication of its 'What We Watched' reports, which provide valuable insights into viewer behavior and content performance. This move has sparked concerns that the company is becoming mor...

item.title: How your winning stock-index fund can turn on a dime — Money Minute Daily | Jul 18
item.description: $3 billion in debt is about to be unleashed by Deutsche Bank AG on behalf of Baker Tilly Advisory Group, in a bold move to refinance private credit loans. This staggering number has significant implications for the accounting services firm, as it looks to shake off its private credit loans and tap into the debt markets. But why is this move so crucial for Baker Tilly? For starters, refinancing its private credit loans could provide the company with more flexible and potentially cheaper funding o...
